_metadata.description: Decision policy layer for PolyJarvis. Encodes the EVALUATION FRAMEWORK for each scientific decision the agent makes, rather than prescribing the action. The Planner consults this when proposing a run_plan.json; the Critic ENFORCES that every decision in the plan addresses each criterion in its policy and cites evidence. Modeled on the electrostatics_decision_guide pattern in polymer_rules.json. 'The agent is free, but the evaluation framework is fixed.'
_metadata.see_also: polymer_rules.json:electrostatics_decision_guide is the prototype for this structure (D-03 policy).

evidence_levels.high: Class-specific MD validation paper reproduces a target property (density or Tg) within ~10%, with DOI.
evidence_levels.medium: Force field validated for a related class or by builder constraint; no class-specific MD Tg/density paper yet.
evidence_levels.low: No validation; choice made by analogy or builder availability only. Reasoned planning REQUIRED.

confidence_gate.description: Controls planning DEPTH. Read class 'confidence' from polymer_rules.json. This is the 'uncertainty-driven planning' mechanism — grounded in an existing field so reproducible fixed-seed runs are never disturbed.
confidence_gate.gate_direction: The plan_mode↔confidence gate is ASYMMETRIC. Only `deterministic` on a non-high/off-table class is a bypass that the Critic must ESCALATE (it claims settled defaults the class has not validated). `reasoned` on a high-confidence class is the SAFE direction — never escalate it; it subjects every decision to full step-3 enforcement instead of fast-path auto-approve. A budget-forced deviation to a non-default ladder on a high class (e.g. PEST [25,50,100] infeasible in a 9.5h wall-clock budget) is exactly what reasoned mode exists for.

high.behavior: Planner transcribes polymer_rules.json defaults verbatim into run_plan.json decided_params (a subset of class keys with identical values). Critic auto-approves (trivial pass, 0 reasoning rounds). Worker prompts are byte-identical to the pre-architecture pipeline. Use fixed seeds from guides/REVISION_PARAMS.md.
high.rationale: These classes are validated; reproducibility outranks re-derivation. Do not re-reason a settled, cited decision.

medium_low_or_offtable.behavior: Planner reasons each decision against its policy below, records evidence/confidence/alternatives, names the dominant uncertainty and an optional uncertainty-reduction probe. Critic challenges; loop until approved or escalate (max 2 critic rounds).
medium_low_or_offtable.applies_to: confidence in {medium, low} OR polymer_class absent from polymer_rules.json.classes

require: mpi>=4 for any PPPM/charged class2 system ON THE GPU PACKAGE (engine=gpu): mpi=1 starves kspace ~75x there — never launch it. This does NOT apply to engine=kokkos, where kspace is GPU-resident and mpi=1 IS the correct PCFF/OPLS default (by_forcefield.pcff). Only flag mpi=1 when engine=gpu.

UA classes (TraPPE: PHYC/PDIE/PSTR) route to GPU package with `neigh yes`, mpi=1, 1 GPU/run (neighbor build is ~71% of the loop and offloads to the GPU; script_generator emits `neigh yes` automatically for use_trappe). This is 3.7x vs CPU mpi=4 and beats KOKKOS on small UA cells (run-0 parity PASS 2026-06-20). NOT pair-only GPU (neigh on CPU ties CPU) and NOT CPU mpi=4 (the old default).
small (<10k-atom) cells use 1 GPU per run, not 4 (comm overhead, GPU underfill)
Sum of mpi across concurrent runs <= 18 physical cores (one GPU-heavy run per GPU)
a multi-GPU pin (gpu_per_run>=2) requires BOTH an estimated cell >~10k atoms AND benchmark support (directional_probe.recommended_by_ff[fam].gpu>=2 on a matching host); otherwise use 1 GPU

when hardware_policy.values_are_benchmarked=false OR the live host (nvidia-smi) does not match directional_probe.measured_on (the field that records where the probe ran — NOT the stale top-level hardware_policy.host, and NOT directional_probe.host which is null and would always mismatch), any non-default D-08 pin MUST be accompanied by a planned hardware_benchmark probe and confidence:low — else keep the by_forcefield default
prefer: when values_are_benchmarked=true AND directional_probe.measured_on matches the live host (nvidia-smi) AND the estimated cell is within ~[0.5x, 2x] of directional_probe.recommended_by_ff[fam].cell_atoms: adopt recommended_by_ff[fam] (engine/gpu/mpi) over the by_forcefield default — this is how measured optima reach runs

otherwise treat directional_probe ABSOLUTE ns/day as a HINT only — but a RATIO measured under uniform contention (all arms niced on the same busy box) plus a run-0 parity PASS is sufficient to flip an engine default, as was done for TraPPE neigh-yes (3.7x ratio, 0.000% parity, 2026-06-20). Do NOT bake in an absolute throughput claim from a contended sweep, and keep values_are_benchmarked=false until a drained-box sweep exists.
hardware.rationale: Hardware config is a function of FF cost structure (UA lj/cut vs PCFF class2+pppm) AND cell size (the GPU-saturation / PPPM all-to-all crossover), so it belongs in policy, not ad-hoc submit-time choice. The live anti-patterns: mpi=1 PCFF on the GPU-package (~2.4 ns/day, ~75x slow — but mpi=1 IS correct for PCFF/OPLS on KOKKOS, where kspace is on the GPU); and UA on the GPU with neigh on the CPU (pair-only offload ties CPU). UA on the GPU with `neigh yes` is the CORRECT default (3.7x), NOT an anti-pattern. The planner emits a D-08_hardware decision grounded in directional_probe + the planned cell size; a deviation from the FF-family default flows to runtime via decided_params (engine/gpu_per_run/mpi_ranks) -> gen_prompt.apply_plan. The conservative gate above keeps the default until a CLEAN (values_are_benchmarked=true), host-matched, in-size benchmark exists.

require: the chosen FF must have parameter coverage for every atom type in the SMILES
force field assigned AFTER polymerization for RadonPy path (never before; cross-stage rule 0)
forcefield.rationale: FF choice dominates property accuracy. A choice without literature_support + validation_data is low-confidence and the Critic must flag it. Alternatives with their known error must be recorded (e.g. GAFF2_mod Tg error >45% for PMMA).

require: charge method consistent with the chosen FF (e.g. TraPPE-UA embeds charges; PCFF uses bond-increment/Gasteiger)
charges.rationale: Apolar UA backbones embed charges in the FF (no QM). Polar all-atom systems need explicit charges. Solubility/CED accuracy degrades for Gasteiger-charged apolar systems — flag charge_method downstream.

require: heteroatom-containing or |q|>0.1e backbones MUST use pppm; pure C/H backbones MAY use lj_cut
electrostatics.rationale: Already encoded as polymer_rules.json:electrostatics_decision_guide. This policy points the Critic at that guide; neglecting long-range Coulomb biases density/Tg by 5-20% for polar polymers.

require: DP above the Fox-Flory plateau (DP>=20) for Tg targets
bulk_modulus targets need DP/nchain above the entanglement-MW threshold (per-class)
cell large enough to avoid self-imaging at the chosen cutoff_A
system_size.rationale: Too-small DP biases Tg low and K high. Too-large wastes GPU budget. The probe option (fast small-cell density screen, ROADMAP E3) reduces finite-size uncertainty cheaply before committing the full cell.

require: check_equilibration_comprehensive overall_pass=True before ANY property extraction (cross-stage rule 2); EXCEPT glassy DP>=30 systems, which use require_glassy (see below); EXCEPT rubbery systems (T_workflow_K > Tg_K), which use require_rubbery (see below)
require_glassy: for regime=glassy AND DP>=30: gate on density plateau within experimental range AND density homogeneity CV<0.25 AND P2 nematic<0.10 AND energy drift/SEM within bounds. The melt chain-self-diffusion metrics (C(t) decay, MSD diffusivity, MSID slope, Rg chain-chain CV) are ADVISORY (logged, non-blocking). overall_pass may be False on these advisory metrics without blocking property extraction. CRITICAL: 'density plateau within experimental range' is a BINDING gate, not a soft warning — convergence (drift/SEM) is necessary but NOT sufficient, because a kinetically trapped (under-annealed) glass converges perfectly at a too-low density. If the 300 K density is below experiment-band (see density_value_binding), the verdict CANNOT be PASS-with-caveats on a bare 'known force-field bias' assertion — route through density_value_binding first.
require_rubbery: for regime=rubbery (T_workflow_K > Tg_K, e.g. PE/PP/PIB/PBD/PI at 300K): gate on density block-SEM < 2% AND density homogeneity CV < 25% AND energy drift/SEM within bounds. The terminal chain-reptation metrics (C(t) full decay, MSD diffusivity, tau_relax, MSID slope, Rg chain-chain CV) are ADVISORY (logged, non-blocking). overall_pass may be False on these reptation-limited metrics without blocking property extraction, provided the density/energy gating criteria pass.
density_value_binding: When the production density at 300 K falls below experiment by more than 5% (glassy) — i.e. outside the pass band, including the former soft -5..-10% 'warning' zone — you MUST run tool:assess_cooling_contraction(melt_data=npt_production_out.data, glass_data=npt_prod300_out.data, exp_density_gcm3, tg_K, t_equil_K) BEFORE accepting the cell, and route on its verdict:

- UNDER_ANNEALED_COOLING (melt density OK, cell under-contracted on cooling): the deficit is frozen-in free volume. Recover via re_melt_slow_recool (see equilibration recovery). Do NOT EXTEND at 300 K (a glass cannot densify below Tg) and do NOT accept as force-field bias.

- MELT_STAGE_DEFICIT (melt density already low) or rubbery: the deficit is at the equilibrium/melt stage — force-field underbinding OR melt-stage under-annealing. Acceptance as 'force-field bias' is permitted ONLY with the assess_cooling_contraction evidence recorded in D-05 (melt gap + contraction shortfall), flagged unresolved, and ideally after one heavy-melt-anneal probe. A bare 'known PCFF bias' caveat is NOT sufficient justification.

- extrapolation_reliable=False (cooling span >300 K, e.g. PEEK): the melt/cooling split is indicative only; lean on the absolute glass-vs-exp density and prefer re_melt_slow_recool before any FF-bias acceptance.

Rationale: convergence (drift/SEM flat) proves the cell stopped moving, not that it stopped at the right density. This routing replaces the soft warning band that let PMMA (-6%, under-annealed cooling) be accepted four times as 'PCFF bias' (2026-06; the 'PCFF PMMA within 2%' claim was itself a Tang2022 misattribution — the real source NkepsuMbitou2025 reaches 1.19 via ~100 ns of melt annealing).
equilibration.rationale: This is the philosophy's 'convince me it is equilibrated' gate — already implemented as check_equilibration_comprehensive. The policy makes it a hard plan success_criterion, not an afterthought. The Validator enforces it after the equil stage.
equilibration.rationale_glassy: For glassy high-MW polymers (DP>=30) melt chain self-diffusion is unattainable on MD timescales (measured tau_relax ~3.6e9 ps for DP=60 PVC; a 1-2 ns extend moves C(t) only ~0.4%->0.5%), so overall_pass=True is unsatisfiable by construction. The 5-cycle high-T anneal substitutes for melt self-diffusion. The metrics that actually bound structural quality for density/Tg/glassy-K_T — density plateau, homogeneity CV, P2 — are gating; the diffusion-limited metrics are advisory. Authorized 2026-06-20 (PVC1 route-back, user-approved). Applies campaign-wide to glassy revision polymers.
equilibration.rationale_rubbery: For rubbery high-MW polymers at T_workflow_K > Tg_K, terminal chain reptation time >> accessible MD simulation time even at high melt temperature (measured tau_relax ~88 ns KWW for DP-120 PE at 550 K; MSD alpha=0.28 subdiffusive). The requested properties — density (packing/cohesion), Tg (segmental kinetics), rubbery-K (P-V EOS volumetric response) — are governed by local packing and cohesive energy, not terminal reptation; each property has its own downstream gate (density block-SEM, Tg bilinear R^2, Murnaghan volume convergence per pressure point). Authorized 2026-06-22 (user-approved, PE1 critic round-2 precedent). Applies campaign-wide to PHYC/PDIE rubbery replicates.
equilibration.rationale_density_value_binding: A converged glassy density that is too low is the signature of kinetic trapping (under-annealing during cooling), NOT automatically force-field bias. The two need different remedies — re-melt+slow-recool vs heavy-melt-anneal — and only assess_cooling_contraction (melt-vs-glass density decomposition) can tell them apart. Before this gate, the equil-checker validated CONVERGENCE only; PMMA/PS/PEEK underdensities were accepted as 'PCFF bias' without testing it. Authorized 2026-06-29 (PMMA forensic: melt 1.055 matches exp, cooled only +5.8% vs +12% expected -> trapped).

require: never report Tg without verifying bilinear fit R^2 (cross-stage rule 3)
T sweep range must bracket experimental_tg_K with margin

every tg_rates_K_per_ns entry must clear tg_min_steps_per_T: N=tg_t_step_K/(rate*dt_fs*1e-6) >= floor (default 200000 steps=200ps@dt=1fs; TraPPE-UA dt=2fs floor=100000). Critic MUST recompute N for EVERY rate independently — never trust the decision's prose arithmetic — and scrutinize the FASTEST rate hardest (that is where the floor gets violated; e.g. PVC4's 200 K/ns = 100ps/T-hold was wrongly stated as clearing the 200ps floor). Retired [40,160,400] gave 50/125ps degenerate fits (cis-PBD2, PEEK2)
tg_protocol.rationale: A too-narrow T range gives R^2<0.80 / <4 bins (known /recover failure mode). Planning the range from experimental_tg_K +/- margin catches this before the sweep runs.

require: glassy bulk_modulus via Murnaghan NPT compression at 300 K (primary — symmetric, cell-size independent; Wu 2019 Comput. Mater. Sci. validated); 3-direction uniaxial deformation fallback if Murnaghan fails (fit_converged=False or B0_prime outside [4, 20]); Born+NVT REMOVED (PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility — K_Born 8-15x too high, Var(P) ~10^7x too high, failed 3/3 pipeline runs PMMA4/PVC1/PEEK1 2026-06-21; see BM_ANALYSIS.md)
rubbery bulk_modulus via Murnaghan EOS sweep at T>Tg (unchanged); volume fluctuation is cross-check only
is_glassy from measured Tg>300K when tg requested, else from experimental_tg_K glassy_hint

for a multi-member class (experimental_tg_K is an OBJECT keyed by member name, e.g. PVNL {PVA:303,PVC:354,PVAc:304}; also PACR, POXI), the planner MUST resolve which member the SMILES is (by substituent — PVNL: *CC(Cl)*=PVC, *CC(O)*=PVA, *CC(OC(C)=O)*=PVAc) and pin THAT member's exp Tg/density/K in the D-04 evidence and assumptions[]. Drive is_glassy off the pinned exp Tg, never the noisy MD Tg — otherwise is_glassy/grading use the wrong target
property_method.rationale: Born+NVT PCFF virial incompatibility confirmed 2026-06-21 across PMMA4, PVC1, PEEK1. Even a 4-ns run (PEEK1) gave K_T=-49.6 GPa. Wu (2019) Comput. Mater. Sci. validates NPT compression as faster and more reliable than volume-fluctuation for polymer K. Murnaghan reuses existing run_bulk_modulus_series tooling; glassy path uses npt_prod300_out.data at 300 K with ±1000 atm symmetric pressure range.

stage_schema_requirements.description: Schema that every planned_stages entry must satisfy. The Critic enforces this in step 3 for reasoned plans; make_deterministic_plan.py satisfies it automatically via the STAGE_TRACK dict.

uncertainty_reduction_probes.description: Optional cheap experiments a reasoned plan may schedule BEFORE the full pipeline to reduce the dominant uncertainty. Each maps to an existing or planned ROADMAP capability.

fast_density_screen.mechanism: small-cell short NPT; compare density to experimental within +/-10%; max 2 FF retries (ROADMAP E3, currently BLOCKED on A1/A2 — record as planned, not executed, until unblocked).

literature_anchor.mechanism: search_ff_literature(smiles, name) -> reference FF + density/Tg targets + DOI (ROADMAP E2).

hardware_benchmark.reduces: hardware_optimum — the mpi/gpu config uncertainty for an off-table FF, an unusually sized cell, or a host whose hardware_policy is not cleanly benchmarked (values_are_benchmarked=false or host mismatch)
hardware_benchmark.mechanism: scripts/calibrate_hardware.py (wrapping scripts/benchmark_hardware.py) sweeps the mpi/gpu matrix on the built .data per template cost class, reports ns/day + Pair/Kspace/Neigh/Comm breakdown, names the winner, and writes directional_probe + by_forcefield (the latter only on a clean uncontended sweep). Runs politely around live runs (idle GPU + spare cores only, niced, sequential).
